subject: Sociology
title: Global Financial Crises
description: A quick, practical guide to Global Financial Crises.

hook.text: In 2008, American banks collapsed and Greek workers lost pensions. Financial contagion respects no borders.

buildup.text: Global financial integration means that a crisis in one country can cascade across the entire world economy.

discovery.text: Deregulation allowed risky lending and speculation. When the bubble burst, governments bailed out banks, not people.

twist.text: Austerity measures punished ordinary citizens for crises created by financial elites. The pain was not shared equally.

climax.text: Global finance connects prosperity and disaster. Economic interdependence means shared risk without shared control.

punchline.text: The gains are private; the losses are public.

quiz.question: Why do financial crises spread globally?
options: Financial integration means crises cascade across borders
